ogTitle: Freemans Waterhole vs French Park: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Freemans Waterhole and French Park.
heading: Freemans Waterhole vs French Park — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Freemans Waterhole, New South Wales and French Park, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Freemans Waterhole has a lower overall climate risk score (42/100) compared to French Park (44/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Freemans Waterhole or French Park safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Freemans Waterhole has a flood risk score of 30/100, while French Park scores 34/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Freemans Waterhole scores 43/100 for bushfire risk. French Park scores 56/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Freemans Waterhole or French Park?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Freemans Waterhole has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 2.12% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
